full_prompt: Design a structural aluminum housing block with a single M10 x 1.5 tapped hole retrofitted with a canonical wire-thread insert (M10 x 1.5 x 1.5D length, 18-8 stainless), sized to carry a rated service tensile load equal to the minimum tensile strength of the grade-8.8 mating bolt without stripping the insert or the aluminum parent material. The parent block is machined from 7075-T6 aluminum (yield 503 MPa, UTS 572 MPa, Young's modulus 71.7 GPa, density 2810 kg/m^3) with overall block size 60 mm x 60 mm x 40 mm thick. The tapped hole is centered on the 60 x 60 face, drilled to the standard STI tap drill size 10.3 mm diameter to an effective depth of 19 mm, then tapped STI M10 x 1.5 and a 1.5D (15 mm) insert installed. A grade 8.8 M10 hex-head bolt mates into the insert, nominal proof load 47.3 kN, nominal UTS-based tensile capacity 58.0 kN (catalog grade 8.8 M10). Load cases: LC1 pure axial tension 47.3 kN (bolt proof load, which is the wire-thread-insert rated service condition for 1.5D length in parent UTS >= 400 MPa per wire-thread-insert design manual (1.5D rated length in parent UTS >= 400 MPa)); LC2 axial tension 58.0 kN (bolt UTS) as the ultimate check; LC3 combined axial 30 kN + transverse shear 10 kN. Pass/fail derived from the wire-thread-insert design manual and general bolted-joint codes: (R1) at LC1 proof load, the thread-engagement shear in the aluminum parent at the insert OD must stay below the aluminum shear-yield Ss = 0.577 * 503 = 290 MPa with SF 1.2, i.e. parent thread shear stress <= 242 MPa; (R2) at LC2 ultimate load, parent thread shear must remain below parent shear-UTS 0.577 * 572 = 330 MPa (no stripping before bolt fractures); (R3) the insert's own thread engagement length 1.5D provides an 18-8 stainless shear capacity per catalog >= bolt UTS load, i.e. insert cannot be the weak link; (R4) block max von Mises under LC3 <= 503 / 1.5 = 335 MPa (SF 1.5 on yield for general structural aluminum); (R5) bolt bearing stress under the head on the aluminum surface <= 0.9 * yield = 453 MPa (bearing check per VDI 2230); (R6) edge distance from insert OD to any free edge of the block >= 2 x insert major diameter = 2 x 10.5 mm = 21 mm (wire-thread-insert edge-distance chart for adjacent-hole / edge-distance in 7075). Manufacturing: CNC mill block; drill and STI-tap per standard wire-thread-insert installation instruction; install insert with prewinder. Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the block with tapped-hole feature (modeled as a through-hole with associated manufacturing callout); report with parent thread shear stress at LC1 and LC2, block max von Mises at LC3, head bearing stress, and compliance with wire-thread-insert minimum edge distance.
short_prompt:
Design a 7075-T6 aluminum structural housing block with a single M10 x 1.5 tapped hole fitted with a wire-thread insert (M10 x 1.5 x 1.5D length, 18-8 stainless), sized to carry the tensile load of the grade 8.8 mating bolt without stripping the insert or the parent material.

Envelope: block 60 mm x 60 mm x 40 mm thick, tapped hole centered on the 60 x 60 face, STI tap-drill 10.3 mm to a 19 mm effective depth, then STI M10 x 1.5 tapped and a 1.5D (15 mm) insert installed. Grade 8.8 M10 bolt: proof load 47.3 kN, UTS-based tensile capacity 58.0 kN.

Load cases.
- LC1 pure axial tension 47.3 kN (bolt proof, the wire-thread-insert rated service condition for 1.5D length in parent UTS >= 400 MPa).
- LC2 axial tension 58.0 kN (bolt UTS) as the ultimate check.
- LC3 combined axial 30 kN + transverse shear 10 kN.

Design safety factors: SF 1.2 on parent shear-yield at LC1; SF 1.5 on yield for general aluminum; bolt-head bearing <= 0.9 * yield per VDI 2230; edge distance from insert OD to any free edge >= 21 mm (2 x insert major diameter).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: VDI 2230 preload, thread shear area, and engagement length formulas are closed-form.

prompt.title: 7075-T6 block with M10 x 1.5 wire-thread insert rated for grade 8.8 bolt
prompt.brief: Design a 7075-T6 block with a single M10 wire-thread 1.5D insert; joint must withstand grade-8.8 M10 bolt proof load 47.3 kN and ultimate load 58.0 kN without parent or insert stripping.

boundary_conditions.block_base: fully fixed at the face opposite the tapped hole
boundary_conditions.bolt_interface: bolt modeled with head bearing + thread engagement pressure

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Wire-thread-insert design manual: 1.5D length in 18-8 stainless >= mating bolt UTS
2 x insert major dia 10.5 per wire-thread-insert edge-distance chart

requirements.governing_standard: Wire-thread-insert design manual + VDI 2230 Part 1 + MIL-I-8846 (for 1.5D length in aluminum)

analysis_types: linear static with bolt-preload + thread pressure

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Static joint qualification; fatigue variant is a separate brief.

notes.numeric_source: Wire-thread-insert design manual (2024) thread shear area tables + engagement length chart for 1.5D inserts in aluminum parent, combined with ISO 898-1 grade 8.8 M10 proof and ultimate loads.

eval_coverage: thread shear vs parent shear check
